title: YT Kids Games observations + RV birthday-trip invite
html:
TL;DR

  • Two-part note from late November 2020: (1) cold observations about the YT Kids Games (Roblox) content genre — "Start with clear summary / Want to be older than they are / Don't like black or brown"; and (2) a draft group-text invite for a December 6 birthday trip in Harris State Beach with the family RV.
  • The kids-content notes read like proto-creator-research: Yahir is reverse-engineering what plays on YouTube Kids — older-than-self aspiration, color-aversion, summary-first format. Six years before AI search optimization thinking, same instinct.
  • The RV-birthday-invite plan loops back to 2020 12 03 rv trip prep (one week later) — confirms the Harris State Beach destination and the "come back Monday Midday" timing.
  • "Don't like black or brown" — flagged as observation-of-platform-bias, not endorsement; reads as Yahir clocking the racial dynamics of the YT Kids algorithm.
  • Roblox + content-format research is one of the earliest what-makes-something-spread notes in the corpus, predating any AI SEO or share of voice thinking by several years.

Source quote

"ROBLOX / Start with clear summary / Want to be older than they are / Don't like black or brown"

"Hey fellas, my birthday is coming up this Friday, and I wanted to have a nice little event planned out for the weekend. The plan is to take my dads RV up to Harris State Beach on Sunday the 6th, and have a good time there. Then come back Monday Midday. What do y'all think?"

Open questions

  • Was Yahir researching YT Kids for a content idea, a school project, or just casual pattern-recognition?
  • Did the Harris State Beach trip happen? Who came? Cross-reference with 2020 12 03 rv trip prep.
  • The "don't like black or brown" observation — is this about character skin tones, color palettes, or thumbnails? Worth annotating if any later notes follow up.

Wikilinks
